design_philosophy.core_loop: Pilot a knight on a flying bird, gain altitude advantage over enemy knights, collide from above to defeat them, collect falling eggs for points, progress through increasingly difficult waves of enemies
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Height-based combat positioning combined with gravity and platforming—players must constantly manage vertical space while avoiding lava and hazards. Cooperative multiplayer can create friendly fire tensions
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Medieval aerial jousting knight commanding a trained bird mount; emphasized through ostrich/stork/buzzard mounts and lance-based collision combat rather than projectiles
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Deliberately avoided overdone space theme in favor of fantasy setting. Rejected standard eight-direction joystick controls in favor of physics-based wing-flapping that directly maps button presses to ascension rate. Uses collision as exclusive combat mechanic rather than weapons or projectiles. Emphasizes cooperative two-player gameplay as core design pillar rather than afterthought

difficulty.lives_system: Multiple lives system; losing a clash or touching lava deducts one life; game ends when all lives exhausted
difficulty.checkpointing: No checkpoints within waves; each wave must be completed to progress. Defeating all enemies in a wave advances to next, more challenging wave
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: No explicit difficulty selection documented. Difficulty escalates automatically through waves with more numerous and challenging enemy types (Bounder, Hunter, Shadow Lord). Pterodactyl enemy spawns after predetermined time, creating time-pressure escalation

progression.unlock_mechanism: Wave-based progression: completing a wave of enemy knights automatically triggers next wave with increased difficulty and enemy variety
replayability_hooks: High score chasing with visible score tracking
Three distinct enemy knight types (Bounder, Hunter, Shadow Lord) requiring different engagement strategies
Cooperative multiplayer allowing friendly fire and competitive dynamic within collaboration
Pterodactyl bonus enemy appearing on timer for additional points
Egg collection mechanic creates risk/reward (collect for points vs. time safety)

world_building_techniques: Archaic English used in onscreen instructions and game-over messages to establish medieval theme
Character differentiation through mount selection (ostrich vs. stork for players; buzzards for enemies)
Environmental hazards with lore implications: indestructible Lava Troll enforces lava as genuine threat, not just damage zone
Enemy knight color-coding establishes faction/type hierarchy without explicit exposition

boss_design.philosophy: No traditional boss encounters. Instead, escalating enemy waves serve progression function. Pterodactyl acts as dynamic challenge element appearing on timer rather than scripted boss fight. Lava Troll functions as environmental boss—indestructible hazard that punishes flight mistakes
notable_bosses: Pterodactyl: timed spawning hazard providing bonus points if defeated
Lava Troll: indestructible environmental antagonist that drags low-flying characters into lava

faction_indicators: Player 1 controls yellow knight on ostrich; Player 2 controls blue knight on stork—clear visual differentiation for multiplayer identification
Enemy knights color-coded by type (Bounder, Hunter, Shadow Lord) establishing hierarchy
Mounted birds as status/faction markers (ostrich/stork = player-controlled; buzzards = enemy-controlled)
danger_signals: Lava at bottom of screen as constant environmental threat with visual and mechanical messaging
Pterodactyl's timed appearance creates escalating danger signal
Shadow Lord enemy type visually distinguished as highest-tier threat
Eggs left from defeated enemies that will hatch into new threats if uncollected—risk escalation signal
environmental_storytelling: Floating rock platforms above lava island establish perilous, isolated arena
Screen wrapping (characters reappear opposite side) suggests enclosed world
Medieval aesthetic through knight, lance, and bird mounts creates cohesive theme
Lava Troll's presence and behavior suggests active antagonistic force in the world

sources_used: tcrf - The Cutting Room Floor: Joust (NES)
